entries.quote: It's important for [Expedia] to be visible on the AI platforms for the remaining third [of business not coming direct to brands]
entries.signal: OTA is doubling down on AI visibility strategy (ChatGPT, Claude integrations) to compete for distribution share amid shifting customer journey — direct material concern for Scapia's travel-fintech positioning
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] Gorin believes AI-mediated search & discovery is becoming a primary customer acquisition channel; Expedia's B2B and brand visibility in LLM responses is now a core competitive lever

entries.quote: The two sides of its business feed one another, and that advertisers like to reach both audiences.
entries.signal: Expedia is doubling down on monetizing both B2B supply partnerships and B2C consumer brands through advertising, signaling a shift toward platform diversification beyond traditional booking commissions; directly relevant to co-brand & loyalty monetization strategies.
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] Gorin is convinced the flywheel between B2B (supply/advertiser reach) and B2C (consumer wallet share) is Expedia's sustainable margin engine, betting against market skeptics who favor pure B2B.

entries.quote: B2B and B2C businesses feed one another, and that advertisers like to reach both audiences
entries.signal: Gorin framing a critical B2B-B2C 'flywheel' at core of growth strategy—could signal partnership openness and advertiser monetization focus that impacts co-branded card positioning
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] Management deeply committed to cross-subsidizing consumer brands with B2B ad revenue and supply-side leverage; B2C recovery is strategic priority

entries.quote: Answer Engine Optimization is the brand's fastest-growing channel...we're encouraged by the mix of new users, conversion and average purchase size
entries.signal: Expedia moving acquisition heavily toward AI-driven channels (ChatGPT, Claude integrations) as alternative to paid search—potential reduced reliance on traditional travel marketing channels
entries.belief: [Inferred · Medium] Treating AI-LLM distribution as core customer acquisition lever, not tactical; signals pivot away from OTA consolidation playbook and toward direct LLM partnerships

entries.quote: Uber is strong in some of the regions where our brands aren't as strong, so that's going to give us access to that demand.
entries.signal: Expedia is leveraging strategic partnerships to expand reach in underserved markets—a model that mirrors co-brand and loyalty partnerships. Direct competitive threat to Scapia's travel card positioning if Expedia monetizes ride-sharing access via loyalty.
entries.belief: [Inferred · High] Management is committed to expanding revenue through partner network effects and adjacent travel services (mobility), not card-exclusive rewards.

entries.quote: People want trust. They don't want an LLM that is plausible. They want something that is certain when it comes to travel
entries.signal: CEO publicly positioning Expedia's data-backed reliability as competitive moat vs. generative AI; frames travel as trust-first category where accuracy/liability matter more than efficiency
entries.belief: [Inferred · Medium] Believes Expedia's first-party data and brand reputation shield it from AI-native disruptors; may reduce urgency for aggressive AI-LLM feature parity if trust narrative sticks with consumers
